name: name
dialog-delete-all-text: Are you sure you want to delete ALL the items?
dialog-delete-text: Are you sure you want to delete this item?
dialog-delete-relationship-text: Are you sure you want to delete this relationship?
dialog-delete-relationship-text-warn: This action will delete all relationships between the two elements.
aria-label-import: Import a new diagram from a file
information-creation-usage: The most important functionality of the application is the ability to create classes and interfaces at will. To do this, just direct the cursor to the + button and select class or interface. After this, a new entity will be created in your visual field on the board.
information-edition-usage-1: For toggle the edition mode in any entity just click it with the right click. This will enable the edition of it's name, attributes and methods, additionally, the button with the trash icon allow the deletion of the entity.
information-edition-usage-2: The add button of the right down corner of the entity allow you to add new relationships, before using it make sure there is another entity you want to pair to the present one. Hover it and it will display the catalogue of relationships available, select one and click the entity you want to connect with.
information-uml-definition: UML (Unified Modeling Language) is a unified modeling language that provides a standard notation for graphically representing software systems and business processes. It is widely used in software engineering for modeling information systems and designing software architectures.
information-relationships-usage-dependen: Dependency is a relationship between two elements where one element (the client) uses or relies on another element (the supplier) for its functionality or behavior.
information-relationships-usage-associat: Association is a structural relationship that represents a connection between two or more classes, indicating that instances of these classes are related to each other.
information-relationships-usage-implemen: Implementation is a relationship between two elements where one element (the client) implements or inherits from another element (the supplier) to gain its functionality or behavior.
information-relationships-usage-inherita: Inheritance is a relationship between two elements where one element (the child) inherits properties and methods from another element (the parent).
information-relationships-usage-aggregat: Aggregation is a relationship between two elements where one element (the whole) contains or is composed of another element (the part).
information-relationships-usage-composit: Composition is a relationship between two elements where one element (the whole) contains or is composed of another element (the part), and the part cannot exist without the whole.
